definitions: When something is done or created for a specific, immediate purpose, rather than as part of a general plan or routine.
On a case-by-case basis, without prior planning or a standing policy.
examples: The committee was formed on an ad-hoc basis to address the urgent issue.
We handle customer complaints on an ad-hoc basis, depending on the specific situation.
Funding for the project was provided on an ad-hoc basis, as needs arose.
